Home › Assessment › Baseline Report

0

Total Baselines

—

Average Age

—

Avg. Family Income

—

Sex ≠ Gender Aware

—

"No means NO" Aware

—

Participated in Activity

Key Findings — Cohort Summary

Loading…

Section A — Profile of the Batch

Age Bands

Education Status (Q9)

Occupation (Q10)

Monthly Family Income

Section B — Gender Norms & Consent

Knowledge of Sex vs. Gender (Q14)

% picking the correct definition — "Sex is biological, gender is social roles / expectations".

Likert — Gender Norm Statements (Q20 · Q21 · Q26 · Q27)

Strongly Agree → Strongly Disagree split for: girls' education matters (Q20), men should always earn (Q21), consent in intimate relationships (Q26), same-sex love acceptance (Q27).

Consent — "No means…" (Q30)

Participation in Gender-Equality Activities (Q48)

Section C — Patriarchy & Human Rights

Perception of Power (Q15)

% who recognise "Men usually have more power" as the correct framing of patriarchy.

Human Rights Awareness (Q13)

Which framings of "human rights" the cohort selected (multi-choice).

Section D — Masculinities & Emotional Management

Toxic Masculinity Recognition (Q16)

Which behaviours the cohort labels as toxic masculinity.

Vignette — Progressive Response (Q44–Q47)

% who chose the egalitarian / acceptance response in each of the 4 prompted scenarios.

"Men should hide emotions" (Q22)

5-point agree / disagree distribution.

Emotion-Handling Strategies (Q31)

Healthy strategies (talk, breathe, write, exercise) vs. suppression (ignore, keep inside, secret).

Section E — Gender-Based Violence

What Counts as Violence? (Q18)

Multi-select: which acts the cohort recognises as violence against women.

"Husband may beat wife if she disobeys" (Q23)

5-point scale. Disagree / Strongly Disagree = progressive.

Witnessed Violence (Last 6 mo) — Q39

Bystander Response — What Did You Do? (Q41)

Multi-select among witnesses. "Ignored" is the doc's dominant pattern.

Section F — Unpaid Care Work

Who Does Cooking / Cleaning / Childcare? (Q17)

The doc's headline insight: typically >90% report "Mother" alone.

"Chores are women's work" (Q24)

5-point scale. Disagree = progressive.

Time on Chores Yesterday (Q34)

Frequency of Helping (Q35)

Section G — Non-Traditional Livelihoods

"Women can do men's jobs" — Q25

5-point scale (bus driver, mechanic, plumber, electrician). Agree = progressive.

"Encourage female sibling to take NTL" — Q42

5-point scale. Agree = progressive.

Home › Case Studies › Add Case Study

Story Metadata

Give this case study a memorable headline and classify it.

What was the story?

Set the scene — who is the member, what context were they in, what happened?

Challenges faced

What barriers, resistance, or hardships did the member encounter?

Actions / Support provided

What did the team / programme do? Trainings, counselling, linkages, advocacy?

Impact / Outcome

What changed for the member, their family, and their community?

Current status / progress

Where is the member today? Sustained gains, next milestones, follow-up needs.

Supporting documents & remarks

Attach photos, consent forms, or other evidence that supports this story.

Key Findings — Cohort Summary

Loading…

Section A — Demographics

Current Education (Q6)

Family Monthly Income

Section B — Gender Norms & Attitudes

Likert Statements — Full Response Distribution (Q80–Q89)

Each bar shows the 5-point Strongly Agree → Strongly Disagree split for each Gender-Norms statement.

Section C — Career & Aspirations

Professions Considered Suitable for Women (Q48)

Bars rank how many leaders named each profession as suitable for women.

Parents' vs Daughter's Education Aspirations (Q44 vs Q45)

Side-by-side: how far parents want her to study (Q44) vs how far she wants to study (Q45).

Open to Non-Traditional Livelihoods (Q50)

Section D — Mobility

Travel to School (Q90)

Unrestricted Local Movement (Q91)

Public Transport Permission (Q92)

Feels Safe Walking Alone — Day (Q96)

Section E — Cyber Safety

Own a Phone? (Q103)

Phone Purpose (Q107)

Online Safety Awareness — Yes / No (Q110 · Q111 · Q113 · Q115)

Coverage of privacy settings, scam reporting, cybercrime laws, and Google-for-education awareness.

Section F — Agency & Leadership

Decisions Leader Can Take at Home (Q56)

Who Decides if She Moves Out for Job/Studies (Q57)

Self-Efficacy Likert — Q99 to Q102

5-point Likert split for "I can achieve goals", "Difficult tasks", "Succeed at endeavors", "Perform under pressure".

Section G — Gender Based Violence

Hitting Women — Accept vs Reject (Q65)

Violence Escalation Awareness (Q70)

What Counts as Violence — Multi-Select (Q68)

How many leaders include each behaviour in their working definition of violence.

Section H — Sexual & Reproductive Health

Information Source on Menstruation (Q72)

What is Used During Menstruation (Q74)

Comfortable Discussing SRH at Home (Q78)

Taboo Practices During Menstruation (Q73)
